stress_signatures: Aroma falls off when water holds above 25C — the volatile oils that define Genovese degrade in heat. Hold temperature before chasing EC.
Leaf coarsens and serrates under high EC; true Genovese leaf is broad, cupped, and tender. Tip burn means the band was crossed.
Early flower spikes blunt the flavour — pinch the moment they appear.
harvest_markers: Ready when the canopy carries large, cupped, deep-green leaves with the sweet anise nose and no purple stem flush.
Cut above a leaf node to drive a second flush; never strip a plant bare.

story.he: בזיליקו ג'נובזה DOP — הבזיליקון המוגן של ליגוריה, עלה רחב ומתוק, הבסיס האמיתי לפסטו ג'נובזה.
story.en: Basilico Genovese DOP — the protected basil of Liguria: a broad, sweet, cupped leaf with a soft anise nose. The only basil a Ligurian pesto is built on, and a name a chef asks for.
